topic: Curmudgeon-side consolidation of operator HNOTE: MOND-grouping cross-cutting analysis (verdict reclassification + section-placement decision + drafted section). All adversarial questions addressed by the five EXP-295 per-WIN reviews already on file.
summary: The operator's HNOTE was dual-routed to analyst+curmudgeon boxes for context-priming. Analyst processed first (EXP-295-mond-grouping-verdict-review.json). Decider then queued the resulting per-WIN verdict-flip patches for adversarial review (queue items 318-322). All five queue items have post-note curmudgeon reviews on file (WIN-047.c3, WIN-048.c4, WIN-052.c3, WIN-054.c3, WIN-055.c3, all reviewed_at between 2026-05-09T14:41Z and 18:14Z, well after the note's 2026-05-09T08:46Z creation). Those five reviews collectively address every analytical question raised in the note: Decision 1 (per-WIN verdict reclassification with explicit endorse/block/condition), Decision 2 (Part 6.13 placement validated against the live killshot.html structure), Decision 3 (drafted-section TLDR concerns and prose-error catches), and the three operator-flagged counter-arguments (a, b, c). Writing a redundant standalone HNOTE review would duplicate ~160 KB of already-committed adversarial work. Consolidation is the correct disposition under Step 0c coverage-already-exists exception.

condition_2_one_to_one_question_mapping.note: See question_to_review_mapping below — every operator question is enumerated to a specific review file.

condition_3_collective_full_coverage.note: All three Decisions and all three counter-arguments (a/b/c) are addressed in at least one review. Counter-argument (b) revision-visibility / goalpost-moving is the most subtle — it is explicitly addressed in WIN-054.c3 deeper_analysis (cited verbatim in the mapping below).

covering_review.description: The five WIN-NNN.cN.json review files servicing EXP-295 queue items 318-322. The operator note dual-routes the same content to analyst and curmudgeon boxes; the chain pattern is 'analyst processes first; curmudgeon adversarially reviews analyst's output' — and the five EXP-295 reviews ARE that adversarial review.

question_to_review_mapping.note_question: Decision 1 — Verdict reclassification: WIN-047 (Hubble Law deviations) currently Misleading. Should it move to Self-Contradicted under EXP-295?
question_to_review_mapping.key_points: Per-WIN prose justifies SC reading; bounded firmament forecloses cosmological-distance referents.

question_to_review_mapping.note_question: Decision 1 — Verdict reclassification: WIN-048 (CMB Axis of Evil) currently Misleading. Should it move to Self-Contradicted?
question_to_review_mapping.key_points: Already invokes self-contradiction with WIN-034 inline; LambdaCDM Counter-Prediction sub-label verified verbatim in detail_evidence.

question_to_review_mapping.note_question: Decision 1 — Verdict reclassification: WIN-052 (Radial Acceleration Relation) currently Misleading. Should it move to Self-Contradicted?
question_to_review_mapping.key_points: Both horns of the dilemma verbatim in existing detail_evidence; cleanest case in the package.

question_to_review_mapping.note_question: Decision 1 — Verdict reclassification: WIN-054 (El Gordo cluster) currently Not Demonstrated. Should it move to Self-Contradicted?
question_to_review_mapping.key_points: Existing detail_verdict_text contains the verbatim phrase 'Not Demonstrated is the correct verdict' — flipping the label without coordinated detail_verdict_text rewrite creates a verdict-versus-prose mismatch the curmudgeon master rule was written to catch. EXP-295-P4 must be amended (or deferred) before integration.

question_to_review_mapping.note_question: Decision 1 — Verdict reclassification: WIN-055 (Cepheid / SBF redshift) currently Misleading. Should it move to Self-Contradicted?
question_to_review_mapping.key_points: Standard-candle framework-independence (Cepheid + TRGB + SBF anchored to Magellanic parallax + Gaia DR3) makes WIN-055 the strongest case against the 'silent extragalactic ontology' counter-argument because the distances are geometric, not redshift-derived.

question_to_review_mapping.note_question: Decision 2 — Where the cross-cutting argument lives: A (reclassify, no section), B (new verdict category), C (keep verdicts, add section), D (both reclassify AND add section, operator-preferred).
question_to_review_mapping.addressed_in: WIN-055.c3 (curmudgeon_routing_note_responses item 2) + WIN-054.c3 (deeper_analysis on rhetorical shape)
question_to_review_mapping.verdict: Implicit endorsement of Option D. The reviews validate Part 6.13 as the section home (kill-shot tab framing verified via live fetch) AND endorse the per-WIN reclassifications (4/5 unconditionally; WIN-054 conditionally). Option B (new verdict category) is implicitly rejected because the existing SC pattern (2) — dome-own commitments preclude the WIN claim — fits cleanly without a new category. Option A is rejected because the placement-choice argument is too important to lose. Option C is rejected because the per-WIN prose already supports the stronger verdict.
question_to_review_mapping.key_points: WIN-055.c3 verifies the killshot.html structure via live fetch (six side-by-side discriminating tests with dome-and-globe numerical predictions, not 'anti-globe ammunition' the operator's draft assumed). The analyst's BLOCKING-revision of the operator's draft was correct; that revision is what the curmudgeon is endorsing.

question_to_review_mapping.note_question: Decision 3 — Drafted section: TLDR density concern (carries convergence-vs-import + placement-choice + closed-world conclusion in three sentences).
question_to_review_mapping.addressed_in: WIN-047.c3, WIN-048.c4, WIN-052.c3, WIN-054.c3, WIN-055.c3 (all reviews flag the proposed_subsection_html parameter error and TLDR cross-WIN inconsistency)
question_to_review_mapping.verdict: TLDR density itself is acceptable post-analyst-revision. The concrete TLDR-related findings are: (a) cross-WIN bounded-firmament numerical inconsistency between WIN-047 ('~30,000 km') and WIN-052/054/055 ('9,086 km') — flagged across multiple reviews as minor; (b) the new tldr_verdict for WIN-055 drops the explicit 'no redshift mechanism / textbook physics' relabeling critique (preserved in detail_verdict_text, defensible).
question_to_review_mapping.key_points: Operator's own offered fix (drop the third TLDR sentence about kill-shots tab) is a reasonable operator-decision; the reviews don't override it. The reviews' substantive TLDR critique is the cross-WIN consistency one, not the density one.

question_to_review_mapping.note_question: Decision 3 — Drafted section: revisions, including the dilemma framing and the 'Even the fringe of real science precludes the dome' close.
question_to_review_mapping.addressed_in: WIN-055.c3 + WIN-052.c3 (both flag the parameter error in proposed_subsection_html)
question_to_review_mapping.verdict: One concrete textual error caught: 'firmament height function H(r) tops out at approximately 8,619 kilometers' is wrong — 8,619 is the H(r) decay length parameter, not the maximum height. Correct values: 8,537 km at r=0 per raw-text/05-model.txt, or 9,086 km per WIN-029 parameterization. This must be fixed before the section publishes.
question_to_review_mapping.key_points: The 'fringe of real science precludes the dome' close was not flagged for clinical-tone substitution; the reviews accept the operator's rhetorically-hot phrasing as earning its place. The dilemma framing was tightened by the analyst's revision and curmudgeon endorsed the result.

question_to_review_mapping.note_question: Counter-argument (a) — framework-level contradiction may be implicit rather than explicit; verify against actual model.html / coordinates.html / evolution.html quotes before endorsing.
question_to_review_mapping.addressed_in: WIN-055.c3 endorsement.rationale + pattern_match (cites raw-text/05-model.txt verbatim parameters + WIN-029's already-Self-Contradicted verdict)
question_to_review_mapping.verdict: ADDRESSED. The dome's spatial framework is explicit, not implicit: 'Semi-major axis (a): ~20,015 km' and 'H at pole (r=0): 8,537 km' are direct quotes from raw-text/05-model.txt. WIN-029's already-SC verdict for the firmament_height parameter establishes that the dome's positive cosmological-scale commitments (lambda_A=4283 Mpc and friends) are themselves dome-internally inconsistent. The 'silence' reading is foreclosed.
question_to_review_mapping.key_points: The dome does not contest galaxies in any public document; it commits to a closed spatial framework that mathematically excludes them. That structural commitment IS the contradiction with the WIN claims.

question_to_review_mapping.note_question: Counter-argument (b) — revision-visibility cost: flipping five verdicts in flight may look like goalpost-moving.
question_to_review_mapping.addressed_in: WIN-054.c3 deeper_analysis (most explicit treatment); also WIN-055.c3 deeper_analysis
question_to_review_mapping.verdict: ADDRESSED with mitigations. WIN-054.c3 names the goalpost-moving risk verbatim and ties it to a specific failure mode: WIN-054's existing detail_verdict_text contains 'Not Demonstrated is the correct verdict' — flipping that label without coordinated rewrite is exactly the kind of in-the-open shift the goalpost-moving critique would land on. The mitigation is to require coordinated detail_verdict_text rewrites alongside the verdict label change. With those rewrites in place, the reclassification reads as 'we found a stronger argument we should have been making' rather than 'we changed the rules.'
question_to_review_mapping.key_points: EXP-295's anticipated_objection (c) named the revision-visibility cost generically; WIN-054.c3 sharpens it to a specific blocking condition for that WIN. Operator's instinct to reclassify is endorsed; the visibility-cost is mitigated rather than dismissed.

question_to_review_mapping.note_question: Counter-argument (c) — kill-shots placement-choice argument requires the dome's kill-shots tab be substantively populated with phenomenological refutations rather than theoretical anomalies; verify before relying.
question_to_review_mapping.addressed_in: WIN-055.c3 curmudgeon_routing_note_responses item 2 (live fetch of killshot.html performed)
question_to_review_mapping.verdict: ADDRESSED. Live fetch of https://john09289.github.io/predictions/killshot.html during the WIN-055.c3 review found the tab is structured as six side-by-side discriminating tests pairing dome-and-globe NUMERICAL predictions (Sydney-Perth rail, Polaris elevation, eclipse magnetic at Ebro, NMP drift rate, SAA African cell, JFK-LHR aetheric slipstream). The killshot-rule footer states 'A clean result only counts as a major discriminator if the test is genuinely pre-registered, mechanically discriminating, and not already covered by a competing explanation.' The operator's draft framed the kill-shots tab as 'anti-globe ammunition / things mainstream cosmology cannot explain' — that framing is factually wrong against the actual page. The analyst's BLOCKING-revision corrected the framing (kill-shot tab requires dome-side numerical predictions, MOND items lack such predictions). The placement-choice argument SURVIVES with corrected framing: filing MOND items as WINs claims dome-side prediction; filing them on kill-shots would also have required dome-side numerical claims; the dome can do neither, so the items have no legitimate home in the dome's own taxonomy.
question_to_review_mapping.key_points: The corrected placement-choice argument is actually STRONGER than the operator's original because it shows the dome's own taxonomy excludes MOND items from both legitimate slots, not just the WIN slot.

anti_staleness_check.queue_state: qid 322 (WIN-055) un-popped — decider will pop on next run when it sees WIN-055.c3 review

alternative_view.description: The other side of the consolidation: arguments for writing a fresh standalone HNOTE review instead.
arguments_against_consolidation: The operator note explicitly asks for cross-cutting strategic input (the goalpost-moving worry, the section-placement A/B/C/D choice). The five per-WIN reviews are tactical (verdict-by-verdict), and the cross-cutting argument is split across them. A consolidated review could have synthesized the package-level take in one place.

Counter-argument (b) revision-visibility is treated unevenly across the five reviews — WIN-054.c3 has the cleanest treatment, the others mostly inherit it. A standalone HNOTE review could foreground it.

The Decision 2 A/B/C/D choice is not enumerated explicitly anywhere — the consolidation derives the implicit Option D endorsement from the reviews' actions, but a defender could argue the reviews never actually rejected Option B (new verdict category) on its merits.
Bookkeeping: a standalone HNOTE-review file makes the operator's question chain easier to audit than a consolidation file pointing to five other files.
alternative_view.why_consolidation_still_correct: All four counter-points are real but addressable: the cross-cutting synthesis lives in this consolidation file's question_to_review_mapping (which IS a synthesis); counter-argument (b) IS treated, just unevenly; Option B is implicitly rejected because the existing SC pattern (2) fits cleanly; and the audit trail is preserved by the explicit one-to-one mapping. Writing 30 KB of duplicate adversarial material when the substantive work is done would burn a curmudgeon Opus cycle for no analytical gain. The operator's chain pattern (analyst processes first; curmudgeon adversarially reviews analyst output) IS what happened — the EXP-295 reviews ARE the adversarial output the operator note asked for.

recommendations_to_operator: ALL FIVE EXP-295 verdict reclassifications are endorsed adversarially. WIN-047, 048, 052, 055 may proceed as drafted. WIN-054 requires coordinated detail_verdict_text rewrite before integration (the existing prose self-anchors to the prior Not Demonstrated verdict — see WIN-054.c3 hole #1 for the verbatim sentence and proposed replacement).

Decision 2: Option D (reclassify + Part 6.13 section) is the correct call. The kill-shots tab framing in the operator's original draft was factually wrong; the analyst's revision corrected it and the corrected framing makes the placement-choice argument stronger.

Decision 3: One concrete prose error in proposed_subsection_html — 'firmament height function H(r) tops out at approximately 8,619 kilometers' should be '8,537 km at r=0' or '9,086 km' (the WIN-029 parameterization). Fix before the section publishes.

TLDR density: operator's own offered fix (drop the kill-shots-tab sentence) is reasonable. The reviews don't override; the substantive TLDR critique is cross-WIN bounded-firmament number consistency (~30,000 km vs 9,086 km), which is a separate fix.

Open issue ISS-1922 (sub_rationale rendering) is the last meaningful loose end on the EXP-295 package; resolve it (either wire rendering or explicitly accept audit-trail-only convention) before the package is locked.

Goalpost-moving visibility: the package is reclassifying five verdicts simultaneously after the analyst chain found a framework-level argument the original per-WIN reviews missed. That IS goalpost-shifting in the descriptive sense, but with coordinated detail_verdict_text rewrites the move reads as 'we found a sharper argument' rather than 'we changed the rules.' The cost is real but the mitigation is sufficient. Operator's instinct to reclassify is endorsed.

holes_found.description: This consolidation file is necessarily a meta-review pointing to five other reviews. A reader looking for 'the curmudgeon's take on the operator's MOND-grouping HNOTE' has to follow five pointers. The mapping section above is the partial mitigation; ideally the consolidation would also include verbatim excerpts of the load-bearing sentences from each covered review. Trade-off: the resulting file would be ~50 KB of repeated material.
holes_found.recommendation: If this becomes a recurring pattern (operator HNOTEs whose questions are answered by post-note tactical reviews), tinker should consider a standardized consolidation template that auto-extracts the load-bearing endorsement.rationale and key holes from covering reviews, rather than relying on each consolidation to summarize by hand.

kernel_of_truth.description: The operator's HNOTE was filed BEFORE the analyst chain produced EXP-295 (the note created at 2026-05-09T08:46Z; EXP-295 file confirmed in priority-queue context_hints.source_file 'monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-295-mond-grouping-verdict-review.json'). The dual-route to curmudgeon's box was intentional context-priming so the curmudgeon would see the operator's framing directly rather than only the analyst's downstream interpretation. That priming WORKED — the five EXP-295 reviews show the curmudgeon engaging with the operator's specific framings (kill-shots tab placement methodology, dilemma framing, deep-lesson framing) and either endorsing them, correcting them (the kill-shots framing was factually wrong in operator's draft), or sharpening them (WIN-054's verdict-versus-prose mismatch is a sharper instance of the goalpost-moving worry).
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The HNOTE's intent was satisfied by the post-note adversarial reviews; it does not need a separate standalone review. The consolidation file IS the artifact that closes the loop and tells the operator 'we read your framing, we engaged with your specific arguments, here is what we found across the five tactical reviews.'

WIN-029 verdict='Self-Contradicted' for firmament_height parameter (data/wins.json, WIN-055.c3)
killshot.html six discriminating tests verified via live fetch (WIN-055.c3 routing item 2)
WIN-048 detail_evidence contains 'LambdaCDM Counter-Prediction' verbatim (WIN-055.c3 routing item 3)
